capability_declaration.evidence: Standardized device models (PV Inverter Model 201, Battery Storage Model 403) define supported operational parameters via published register maps; capabilities are identified through static hardware model ID codes.
capability_declaration.rationale: Provides structural capability mapping through equipment profile registers rather than dynamic service declaration or negotiation mechanisms typical of modern autonomous agent frameworks.

bounded_authority.evidence: Register ranges in models specify operational bounds and setpoint ceilings for DER commands; however lacks explicit mechanisms for role-based access control or dynamic authority scoping tailored to autonomous agents.
bounded_authority.rationale: Operational boundaries are defined per equipment model but lack granular agent-level permission delegation or revocation frameworks required for unattended AI operations.

decision_auditability.evidence: Standard defines command/response sequences that inherently generate communication logs, though does not mandate specific audit trails or decision attribution mechanisms tailored to autonomous agents within the protocol specification itself.
decision_auditability.rationale: Transactional nature enables retrospective analysis when paired with external SCADA/logging systems lacking native agent-specific audit mandates for AI-driven control loops in the standard text.

data_governance.evidence: Register layouts define exactly which operational/telemetry data fields are exposed by DER hardware models but completely omits provisions for ownership attribution, user consent mechanisms or lifecycle governance.
data_governance.rationale: Establishes technical boundaries of what metadata is exchanged per device model while leaving all higher-level privacy rights and retention obligations unaddressed entirely.
